mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.5% signals rising inflation pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite inflation spikes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal a tightening labor market that boosts corporate earnings and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >40% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE meet trigger conditions, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO signals reduced industrial output that weakens corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >50% of triggered instances, or INDPRO declines fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO above its 12-month moving average indicates robust industrial activity that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >35% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA, or rolling 5d IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE below 5% indicates a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and reduces corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of instances where UNRATE is below 5%, or the coefficient on UNRATE in regressions is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in HOUST above 10% signals strong housing demand that boosts construction activity and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >40% of instances where HOUST meets the trigger condition, or the signal's IC falls below 0.07 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDPC1 and stable CPIAUCSL indicates strong economic demand without inflationary pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >30% of instances where both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL meet trigger conditions, or the interaction term's coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).
